Long Island some finest beaches world.
What compare beauty with Main Beach East Hampton?
Malibu?
Cannes?
Miami?
Those beaches thin, puny strips sand.
Incredibly overrated.
They could Long Island beach same time there would plenty shoreline spare.
never tired looking beautiful beach East Hampton.
when stare look long enough, always think back what used When grew they say, "limited circumstances," trip beach best celebration line "The best things life free."
grew West Street Brooklyn, subway stop (five minutes) from Coney Island.
Beach train (now called behind tiny house when kid.
Every time train rolled into station entire house would shake.
night would listen train come friendly, familiar sound little kid: meant people were coming home.
summer would take little brother Coney Island just about every day.
like traveling
When walked subway, some incredible smells fought with each other into your nose.
first smell clams being squirted with lemon.
then there smell ice-cold beer foaming glass clam that promenade subway terminal.
walked across street, smelled sweetness cotton candy seconds later smelled garlic spices those sizzling Nathan's dogs that made your mouth water.
time boardwalk, were starving reaching your homemade sandwiches sneak bite.
could beach walking onto boardwalk under years later Drifters would tell world about wonders that could found "Under Boardwalk.")
Walking under faster onto beach that's always went.
braved cold clammy sand that hadn't felt sunlight years.
gingerly stepped over (while still managing sneak peek teenage couples were passionately "making out" blankets semi-privacy that could only found under boardwalk.
They pretended they were invisible.
very young knew enough along with pretense.
walk beach joke.
There seemed millions people beach; consequently, there beach.
stepped beach blanket after another.
Finally, staked claim parked blanket, touching four other blankets, rushed water.
honest, water Coney Island just slightly cleaner than Ganges India.
recent spill disaster triggered sense memory that hadn't thought about many years.
years after World water Coney Island filled with chunks that blackened feet.
remember father telling that probably came from ships that been blown nearby during war.
remember wondering ours theirs.
smell suntan oil, remember staying water hours fight waves.
Invariably, mother would call because "your lips turning blue."
never came into water.
just joined other mothers were standing shore "blue patrol."
When time came home, always begged another half-hour.
They always gave
one-stop ride home, would rush front first stand toes open window.
cool hitting sunburned face would feel wonderful.
favorite line movie from Atlantic City.
very Burt Lancaster trying impress very young Susan Sarandon.
They're looking Atlantic Ocean.
says, "It's very beautiful."
says, "Yes."

should have seen days."
children have same ocean days.
they don't have noise subway trains every night.

beach they walk East Hampton spotlessly clean well maintained.
